reviews.review_description: I’ve had a bit of fun with this one. The graphics are tight, a bit cartoonish but very well made. The gameplay is simple but has some depth to it. There are puzzles to solve, a bit of crafting that you unlock by acquiring currency and which you can use to upgrade your powers. The gameplay is basically slingshotting rats, using powers to give you an edge, and finally big bosses that you have to dodge from and shoot back, while rats are all over trying to get you. The game could use with some additional sound design, more crafting, and better story exposition, but overall it’s a simple game that will grab anyone’s attention and imagination immediately. You can play it sitting too which was nice.

So, when I saw the game for the first time, I was really excited! The design, graphics and style looked fantastic (and they still do, tbh). the idea is interesting to say the least, the rats running really funny, I like that even in the early version of the game there are already a lot of different enemies. 
I like the simple puzzles in there, they make you think you're smart, even tho you aren't xD.
But also... The slingshot is so annoying... Many times when you shoot, it will spin around and around, so you can never grab it again... It's not a big problem, when you're just killing harmless rats, but with bosses, whose mechanics rely on the slingshot, it's a huge deal beaker... Like the bomb rats with the chest boss and the rat kind's sword attack, I died a lot there just because I couldn't grab it. So... The rat kind is bugged... 
There is an attack when he drinks a bottle of alcohol, and then uses a torch to burn you, doesn't matter if you switch platforms on time, you will still get damaged, which sucks, because the health decreases slowly on its own, and since the attack is inevitable, and deals a lot of damage, by the time i reach the last phase, i die from natural causes...

Great little game. 

Mechanics take a little getting used to and a clear tutorial would be great to initially get going. 

I have given the game 4 stars only for the fact this it’s not fully supportive of playing in seated mode, few tiny tweaks and it’s a 5 star game from me. 

It can become very addictive trying to find all them rats and love the fact it’s puzzle based game also just to keep things fresh. 

Great little game

Review Rat It
System: Quest Pro
4-Graphics
1-Replayability
2-Mechanics
4-Bugs
2-Entertainment value
1-Wow factor
5-Vr sickness

2.7 - overall

Rat it starts out quite entertaining and getting you psyched up to pop off some rats, but quickly dies off to poor movement and overwhelming repetition.  The graphics are great even though cartoonish, but the movement was a killer for me personally.

In todays world of gaming, especially in VR, who wants to teleport from circle to circle to circle on the floor.  Being able to walk around the rooms freely is the first and most immediate thing that needs to be addressed.  

If you can get passed the poor movement, the game itself had some real possibilities, but even that fell quickly by the wayside.  Knocking off rats with a slingshot started off quite fun but by the time you’ve killed 50-100 rats boredom quickly sets in, not to mention “sling shot elbow” LOL.  

However with some changes this game could absolutely be alot of fun.  If they fix the movement, cut down the number of rats, add some other things to shoot, and throw in some powerups, and make bottles and other objects breakable it would completely change the hum drum dynamics to something alot of people would enjoy.  I hope they do as i’d love a better opportunity to knock off some pesky rats.

The Daywalker

It might just be me but the game isn't clear about what you have to shoot and how many shots you get and the intro video is really unclear, wish there was more of a tutorial than the short one you get.

This vr game offers a really cool experience with its realistic rat killing action. The graphics are ok and the gameplay is pretty fun but repetitive , making it a cool adventure for rat killing enthusiasts. However, a bit more variety in objectives could enhance the overall experience.

Visuals and ambient sounds are great. A lot of detail has gone into this game. Hopefully free movement will be available as an option and not just teleport. I was really surprised at how accurate the slingshot was. It seemed true to aim. I think this will be a great game for the young and old. Can't wait to see more!

Rat It Plague Hunter is a short, charming shooter game that does it right. It goes beyond the normal VR shooter game to incorperate many additional fun elements that make it worth a play.

The graphics are okay for a standalone Quest game. It's not very graphic, but the graphics remind me of an nostolgic N64 game. However, to compliment this, it would have been nice if they included catchy 8bit music during the game. I'm assuming they'll add this in the final version.

The gameplay is very fun, since it goes beyond just a normal VR shooter game. For example, there are mini-puzzles where you have to slingshot marbles at specific targets to solve them. Not spoiling too much there's a puzzles where you have to shoot the targets in the correct order based off a picture in another location. In addition to this, they added an upgrade system where you can upgrade your five types of marbles (e.g. flame, lure etc) to make them more powerful. This is a nice touch that goes beyond the normal VR shooter. Finally, they have classic N64 style bosses. Need I say more. :)

My only complaint would be that it is rather short, being about 1 to 2 hours depending on how skilled you are and if you want all the upgrades. However, for the price, it is certainly worth a play as its one of the better VR shooters that I have played. Overall a great game!

I like the concept but don't get the story behind it the way you have to kill the rats is quite fun and satisfying on the second or third level I fell through the map I don't know what happened there but overall it's a nice game I would recommend it.

reviews.review_title: Great potential no the best right now
reviews.review_description: This game has a lot of potential to be better after a few bug fixes and some better movement mechanics I think it will be really good one of the bugs I came across was sometimes the rats just don’t spawn like on the part after you hit the gears and drop the skeletons I just got stuck on 8 rats left I am not sure if it is intentional and I need to do some more puzzles but I can not get past that part

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. May I ask which level did you stuck on?

reviews.review_description:
At the core of this game is a super fun concept that makes you feel like the Katniss Everdeen of pirates hunting rats in a wonderfully cartoon-ish ship. Grabbing the slingshot and sniping the rats from across the room can be really satisfying. The puzzles are unique and solvable, without being too easy.

However, this game does need some more polish before it's ready (the game is in early access as of writing this review). Here are the critiques I believe are relevant to this review:

- Lack of help/hints: there were a few times where I had no clue what the game wanted from me in order to continue. There was no real way to identify where the last rat or two are hiding without scouring the room corner to corner, which may result in running out of time. It was also hard for me to tell why the first door was locked, until I realized it was a puzzle.

- UI is a little "clunky": it just didn't feel intuitive to switch to different marbles and cancel shots when I needed to. Thankfully this is something that isn't a game breaker, because it's part of the polish phase and has little to do with gameplay at the core.

- Length of play: I'm not sure about plans to add more levels or different things to do, but at the moment the game feels a bit short. I feel like it's missing a "free range" or "arcade" mode where you can just practice your shooting.

All things considered, I think this game will be a blast when it is finished and would be worth picking up! If you like bow and arrow style games, this is for you. If you just enjoy a bit of arcade shooting and puzzles, this is for you.

I love the style of this game, it reminds me a little of Sea of Thieves. Even though it is very stylised, it is very immersive with impressive lighting and high Res textures.

I really liked the puzzle elements of the little levels in-between the main room levels and wished there would be more of these. 

The mechanic of using the slingshot to kill the rats is fun at first, but I found myself getting really annoyed when it takes around 8 shots to kill some of the rats with more health.

The main rat boss is extremely difficult to defeat due to poor game mechanics. You have to teleport to predetermined locations and the on screen prompt that allows you to do so gives you such a small margin of time before the boss attacks you with a fatal attack, that it was impossible for me to complete. This left me extremely annoyed at having to repeat the same fight over and over and getting nowhere.

My biggest gripe for the game would be that the health system mechanics aren't explained at all and there are no instructions on what you actually need to do once the game starts. After a few failed attempts on one of the levels I realised that the rats with the pumpkin head gives you more life (which equals time in this game), so it made more sense to not kill these straight away as no 'overhealth' is given.

I enjoyed my experience with this game but I don't think that it's one that I would be coming back to and replaying, although it would be fun to showcase to friends and family.

The game has some clearly will defined qualities. The art style is *amazing* I would love more games that looked like this but the gameplay is not quite there. 

Having finite spots you can move to is not fun. I would like to be able to move around the world more easily without actually moving around in my playspace. 

I think as much more skill as a slingshot requires I would personally prefer a weapon that did not require two hands to aim for a more arcade shooter feel because that's effectively what this game is.

Also please don't put an exit game button in the settings menu. Just make it a back to title menu button for a better UX.

I honestly didn’t play to much but from what I did play the movement can be annoying 
I feel like if you could teleport to any area instead of selected areas things would be a lot easier other than that it’s a fun game but it definitely has some bugs.

The game doesn't hold your hand in terms of tutorial but it would be better off with one. I didn't know that if you pull your string back far enough, the reticle will get smaller to aim better. I didn't know you had to hit random crates to find more rats. It also needs to be better at telling the players which objects are breakable and which are not. Also, in the first zone I died twice not knowing what to do. I had to kill all the rats within a time limit but the game didn't do a good job of explaining that.

It does have really good Torchlight implementation. The puzzles were intuitive and the sling mechanism was good. 

If it gets more polish and tutorial, this would be a fun game.

I'm giving it four stars for the potential I see in it, but as it stands, it's a bit too repetitive for me without enough options to make the game exciting enough to come back to. I look forward to see how it develops.

While the game is fun and there is lots of little hidden things and events to trigger it is sadly short currently. Worth the price for what you get, though more modes added would benefit the upgrading of marbles. The boss fights were annoying at first, as well as some of the levels. Once you understand where to be and what to look for, and fully utilize the special marbles it becomes quite fun. I hope more is added in the future!

reviews.review_title: Fun game but can get old & repetitive
reviews.review_description: For what it’s worth, not a bad game! It was fun to use a slingshot in VR and the graphics were pretty good for the most part. I wish it had a bit more of a tutorial or helpful hints along the way when you needed to learn something new. The slingshot would glitch a little here or there and the rats were sometimes a bit hard to hit based on some aiming offsets. Game is on the shorter end, but has some good features overall.

I played the first level and could barely get past it because I didn’t know what to hit. I feel the first level could just be a tutorial and show you what you can hit and how to use things. I also feel like the movement is a little lazy. It’s a little hard to play due to the bad movement and the dark atmosphere. But overall it’s a decent game I see that the devs have tried their hardest and it is only in its beta.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for your feedback. The first level it is design for the tutorial level. It have show you the basic gameplay and the movement method. May I ask which part make you so confuse for the gameplay?

reviews.review_description: It is too dark. Game consists only of shooting tiny rats, which gets boring fast. Also level 1 has a tight timer so good luck getting past it.
